zh-tw.title: 婚禮金句
zh-tw.description: 從古風祝賀、現代俏皮到真摯告白，精選婚禮致詞、紅包賀詞、誓詞與祝福金句——適合伴郎伴娘、來賓、長輩、新人本人，每一句都能直接抄走

en.title: Wedding Quotes
en.description: Heartfelt vows, hilarious toasts, and timeless blessings — wedding quotes you can borrow for speeches, cards, and that moment when everyone's looking at you and you forgot what you were going to say

items.sourceNote: 古風成語 register，重新組合改寫

zh-tw.content:
鸞鳳和鳴，花好月圓。

願你們的日子，
是琴瑟輕響、燈火常亮，
一年一年，把平凡過成詩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 紅包寫得太短像敷衍、太長又寫不下——這版四字成語開頭、口語化收尾，剛好符合紅包袋的字數，又比「百年好合」多一點誠意
variations: 願鸞鳳和鳴，願花好月圓，願你們以後的每一頓晚餐都還是想坐在彼此對面。

en.content:
May your days be long, your laughter louder, and your home the kind of place where everyone wants to come back to.

Congratulations.
en.editorNote: A clean three-line blessing for a card or red envelope when you don't have a specific story but want it to feel sincere
variations: May your years together be long, your laughter loud, and your home the soft place you both keep choosing to come back to.

items.sourceNote: 現代俏皮 register（遊戲闖關比喻），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
戀愛是新手村。
同居是支線任務。
結婚就是正式進入主線劇情了。

恭喜你們解鎖「婚姻」，
下一關是「裝潢吵架」，
通關獎勵是更愛對方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合年輕朋友當伴郎伴娘致詞、IG發文，或在喜宴上敬酒前帶一句——遊戲比喻比官腔祝賀好笑十倍
variations: 婚姻是大家都在玩、但沒有人讀過攻略本的一款遊戲，恭喜你們選了一個值得一起破關的隊友。

en.content:
Dating: tutorial level.
Moving in: side quest.
Marriage: you've officially unlocked the main story.

Congrats on the upgrade.
The next boss fight is "deciding on the couch."
The reward is loving each other more.
en.editorNote: Plays well for a best man / maid of honor opener at a younger crowd, or as an Instagram caption — gaming metaphor lands harder than formal congratulations
variations: Marriage is the game everyone's playing without reading the manual. Congrats on choosing a teammate worth speedrunning life with.

items.sourceNote: 誓詞風 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你的，
不是「永遠不吵架」、
不是「一定不會讓你失望」。

我答應你的是：
吵完還會回來說對不起，
失望過後還是選擇你，
以後的每一個明天，都還想跟你過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞最忌空泛——這版避開「永遠不」的承諾陷阱，改用「會回來」的具體行動，比『一輩子愛你』可信一百倍
variations: 我不會答應你完美，但我會答應你誠實——誠實地修補我們會弄壞的東西，誠實地一直選擇你。

en.content:
I'm not promising you we'll never fight.
I'm not promising I'll never let you down.

What I'm promising is this:
I'll come back after the fight.
I'll keep choosing you after the disappointment.
And every tomorrow we get, I still want to spend it with you.
en.editorNote: The strongest vows are the specific ones. This avoids the 'forever / always / never' trap and replaces it with promises you can actually keep
variations: I'm not promising perfect. I'm promising honest — honest repair of the things we'll break, honest choosing of you, again and again.

items.sourceNote: 幽默致詞 register（Erma Bombeck 風格），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
婚姻沒有保固。

如果你想要保固，
建議你去買冰箱。

但是冰箱不會在你難過的時候抱你，
所以還是結婚比較划算。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞前段破冰用——先笑場再轉煽情，全場接受度100%。可以接「我們今天看到的這對新人，雖然沒有保固，但有比保固更值得的東西」延伸
variations: 婚姻不保證任何事情，唯一保證的是：你身邊那個人，會跟你一起面對所有沒被保證的部分。

en.content:
Marriage comes with no warranty.

If you want a warranty,
buy a refrigerator.

But a refrigerator won't hug you when you're sad,
so marriage still wins.
en.editorNote: Classic toast opener — get the laugh first, then pivot to sincerity. Works for best man, maid of honor, or any speech that needs to break the formal ice
variations: Marriage offers zero guarantees. The only thing it offers is the person standing next to you, choosing to face all the un-guaranteed parts together.

items.sourceNote: 古風 register，重新組合

zh-tw.content:
百年好合，永結同心。
舉案齊眉，相敬如賓。

願你們攜手走過的，
不只是熱鬧的今天，
還有很多很多個，
安靜也甜的明天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 長輩致詞、長官致詞最安全的套路——前兩句正式四字成語、後段口語化收尾，避免「太古老」也避免「太隨便」
variations: 百年好合是祝福，永結同心是承諾，舉案齊眉是日常——願你們三樣都擁有。

en.content:
May your love be long, your respect mutual, and your laughter daily.

Not just on this loud, beautiful day —
but on all the quieter ones that come after.
en.editorNote: Safe register for parents, bosses, and senior speakers — formal blessing followed by a softer closing. Won't offend anyone, won't bore anyone
variations: Long love, daily laughter, mutual respect — three blessings for three lifetimes of marriage.

items.sourceNote: 電影金句 register（Princess Bride / Frozen 改編風格），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
認識他們很多年了。

我可以告訴你，
他們不是電影裡那種完美的愛情。

他們是真的版本：
會吵架、會冷戰、
但每一次都還是先伸手的那種。

那比完美更難得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 好朋友當伴郎伴娘致詞最有殺傷力的開場——比「他們很相愛」更有畫面感，全場會點頭認同
variations: 完美的愛情是電影演的，他們的愛情是真實的——會出狀況，但每次都會修好，這種比完美更值得敬一杯。

en.content:
I've known these two for years.

Let me tell you, they're not the movie kind of love.

They're the real kind:
they argue, they go quiet,
but they're always the first to reach back.

And honestly? That's harder to find than perfect.
en.editorNote: The strongest opener for a close-friend speech — replaces 'they love each other so much' with a specific, visible quality everyone in the room can verify
variations: Perfect love is what movies are about. Real love is what these two have — it breaks, it gets fixed, and it keeps choosing the fixing. Cheers to that.

items.sourceNote: 現代紅包賀詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

願你們有理由的時候慶祝，
沒理由的時候也慶祝。

婚姻最好的部分，
是給生活找慶祝的藉口。
zh-tw.editorNote: 紅包賀詞最難寫的不是長度、是「不老套」——這版避開「百年好合」直接給一個更有溫度的祝福，記得住、抄不爛
variations: 新婚快樂——願你們把生活過成節日，不用等理由。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

May you celebrate the big things,
the small things,
and the things that aren't really 'things' at all.

That's the best part of marriage —
you get a built-in reason to celebrate everything.
en.editorNote: A red-envelope or card message that doesn't sound like a Hallmark template — short, warm, and quotable
variations: Happy wedding day — may your years be one long, ordinary, joyful excuse to celebrate.

items.sourceNote: Rainer Maria Rilke 風格 改編，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
愛情不是兩個人變成一個。

是兩個完整的人，
選擇站在彼此旁邊，
保護對方的那部分孤獨，
也允許對方有一個人靜靜的時候。

這就是我看到你們在做的事情。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞 / 誓詞中最高級的版本——避開「你完整了我」這種把另一半當補丁的說法，改成「我們各自完整、選擇靠近」更現代、也更健康
variations: 最好的愛情，不是把對方當作另一半，是把對方當作另一個完整的人，然後決定肩並肩走下去。

en.content:
Love isn't two people becoming one.

It's two whole people
choosing to stand beside each other,
protecting each other's solitude,
letting each other have the moments where they're quietly themselves.

That's what I've watched you two do.
en.editorNote: The highest-register vow or speech version — replaces 'you complete me' (which treats your partner as a missing piece) with something modern, healthier, and more truthful
variations: The best love isn't completion — it's two whole people choosing, every day, to walk side by side.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（伴郎/伴娘吐槽），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他第一次跟我講她的時候，
講了四十分鐘。

我聽完只說了一句：
「兄弟，那你就娶她吧。」

結果他真的娶了。
所以接下來的婚姻問題，
我也算共犯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎致詞神級開場——用「我害他結婚」當笑點，後面再轉「但我從不後悔慫恿你」就是滿分敬酒詞
variations: 我承認，當初我推了他一把。看到今天的場面，我覺得我這一推，是這輩子做過最對的事情。

en.content:
The first time he told me about her,
he talked for forty minutes straight.

I just said: "Bro, then marry her."

And he did.
So legally, I'm at least 30% responsible
for everything that happens from here.
en.editorNote: Best-man gold — open with self-incrimination, pivot to 'and it's the best thing I've ever pushed him into.' Reliable laugh, reliable warmth
variations: I take partial credit for this whole marriage. He talked about her for forty minutes, I told him to marry her, and here we are. Best advice I never knew I gave.

items.sourceNote: 古風 + 現代混搭 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
古人說「執子之手，與子偕老」。

白話的版本是：
以後牽手過馬路、
一起去看醫生、
半夜起來上廁所順便幫對方蓋被子——

這些事情，做一輩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把成語翻譯成生活——適合長輩或有點文青的朋友致詞，比單純引用古文更有溫度，記憶點也比現代俏皮版更深
variations: 「執子之手」是詩；牽著對方過馬路、半夜幫對方蓋被子，是這首詩寫了一輩子的版本。

en.content:
There's an old line: "To hold your hand and grow old with you."

The modern translation:
holding hands at crosswalks,
going to doctor's appointments together,
getting up at 2 a.m. and pulling the blanket back over them.

Do that. For a lifetime.
en.editorNote: Translates a classical line into ordinary, visible life — works for parents, older speakers, or anyone who wants more weight than a one-liner can hold
variations: 'Grow old together' is the poem. Pulling the blanket back over them at 2 a.m. is the poem, written daily, for fifty years.

items.sourceNote: 麻將/桌遊比喻，台灣本地 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
婚姻有點像打麻將。

你不會永遠摸到好牌，
但只要旁邊那個人不換，
就算自摸也好、放槍也好，
總是同一桌的人。

恭喜你們開了一桌只屬於你們的局。
zh-tw.editorNote: 台灣本地比喻效果一流——麻將是大家都懂的場景，比西式比喻更有共鳴，可在敬酒時用，全桌會點頭
variations: 婚姻像麻將，重點不是你摸到什麼牌，是跟你打到天亮的還是同一個人。

en.content:
Marriage is a little like a long card game.

You don't always get good hands.
But as long as the person across from you doesn't change,
every win, every loss, every unlucky round —
you're still at the same table.

Congrats on opening a table that's just yours.
en.editorNote: A localized metaphor that works internationally — every culture has its long, slow, sit-around-the-table game; the warmth translates
variations: Marriage isn't about the hands you're dealt. It's about whether the same person stays at the table with you all night.

items.sourceNote: 現代創意祝福 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
願你們的Wi-Fi訊號永遠滿格，
冰箱永遠有零食，
吵架永遠不過夜，
愛永遠比帳單先到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 四個排比句、一句一個現代生活畫面——適合紅包賀詞、IG發文、賓客留言本，年輕人看了會笑、長輩看了會懂
variations: 願你們有滿格Wi-Fi、有零食常備、有不過夜的吵架，和一個比明天還可靠的彼此。

en.content:
May your Wi-Fi always be strong,
your fridge always be full,
your fights never last past bedtime,
and your love always arrive before the bills do.
en.editorNote: Four parallel lines, four modern-life images — for a card, an Instagram caption, or a guestbook signature. Funny enough to share, warm enough to mean it
variations: Strong Wi-Fi, full fridge, short fights, deep love. That's the whole recipe.

items.sourceNote: 感人致詞 register（父母 / 長輩視角），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
今天我把女兒交給你。

不是因為她不再屬於我，
是因為她長大了，
選了一個她願意把自己交出去的人。

請你接住她的時候，
也記得她以前是怎麼被接住的——
認真地、輕輕地、不弄丟任何一塊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 父親 / 母親致詞最會哭點的一段——比「請你好好照顧她」更立體，把「接住」這個動作講出來，全場必哭
variations: 我女兒不是嫁給你，是選了你。請你也每天選她一次。

en.content:
Today I'm handing my daughter to you.

Not because she stops being mine —
but because she grew into someone
who chose a person she trusts to hold her.

When you catch her,
please remember how she was carried before:
carefully, gently, with nothing ever dropped.
en.editorNote: Father-of-the-bride / mother-of-the-bride speech — the most powerful version uses the word 'catch,' not 'take care of.' Tears guaranteed
variations: She isn't being given to you — she chose you. Please choose her back, every single day.

items.sourceNote: 結尾敬酒 / 全場舉杯 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
敬今天。
敬以後每一個普通的星期三晚上。
敬那些不會被拍下來、
但你們會記一輩子的時刻。

乾杯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞最後舉杯的萬用結尾——避開「祝你們白頭偕老」的老套，把祝福投到「普通的星期三晚上」，畫面感比口號強十倍
variations: 敬今天，敬明天，敬一萬個普通而幸運的明天。乾杯。

en.content:
To today.
To every ordinary Wednesday night that comes after.
To the moments no one's filming
but you'll both remember forever.

Cheers.
en.editorNote: Universal speech-closer — skips the 'happily ever after' cliché and lands the toast on 'ordinary Wednesdays,' which is what marriage actually is
variations: To today, to tomorrow, and to ten thousand quiet, lucky tomorrows after that. Cheers.

items.sourceNote: Saint-Exupéry 「望向同一個方向」概念，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
愛情不是一直盯著對方看。

是你們站在一起，
望向同一個方向，
看見同一片風景，
然後說：
「對，我想跟你一起去那裡。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把法國名言翻成台灣人聽得進去的版本——適合致詞中段轉煽情的時候，比直接引用洋文更有溫度，長輩晚輩都能接收
variations: 最好的伴侶，不是天天看你的人，是願意跟你看同一個遠方的人。

en.content:
Love isn't staring at each other forever.

It's standing side by side,
looking at the same horizon,
seeing the same view,
and saying:
"Yeah. I want to go there with you."
en.editorNote: A modern, plain-spoken take on the classic 'looking in the same direction' line — works as the emotional pivot in a friend's or sibling's toast
variations: The best partner isn't the one who watches you forever — it's the one who looks at the same distance with you and chooses to walk toward it.

items.sourceNote: 新郎 / 新娘致謝 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
謝謝爸爸媽媽，把我養成一個敢去愛人的人。
謝謝今天每一位來的朋友，
讓我們的開始有那麼多人見證。

最後謝謝你——
謝謝你讓我從今天開始，
有一個正式的理由，
每天都更喜歡你一點點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 新人答謝詞萬用模板——三段式：謝家人、謝賓客、謝對方。最後一句是記憶點，把「結婚」說成「每天更喜歡一點的正式理由」，全場會被打動
variations: 謝謝你願意跟我簽一張，需要一輩子才能讀完的約。

en.content:
Thank you to my parents, for raising someone brave enough to love.
Thank you to everyone who came today —
our beginning has more witnesses because of you.

And finally, thank you —
for giving me, starting today,
an official reason
to like you a little more every morning.
en.editorNote: A clean three-beat thank-you for the bride/groom: family, guests, partner. The closing line — 'an official reason to like you more every morning' — is the keeper
variations: Thank you for signing a contract with me that takes a lifetime to read. I plan to read every page.

items.sourceNote: 「夠現代、夠老派」對比 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
願你們的愛情，
夠現代——
受得了世界一直在變。

夠老派——
還是會手寫卡片、
還是會記得結婚紀念日、
還是會在停電的時候，
點一根蠟燭給對方看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「現代 + 老派」二元對比是萬用句型——適合紅包賀詞、賓客留言本，文青朋友尤其買單
variations: 願你們的愛夠新，跟得上時代；夠舊，記得手寫卡片和紀念日。

en.content:
May your love be modern enough
to survive a world that won't stop changing.

And old-fashioned enough
to still write the card by hand,
still remember the anniversary,
still light a candle for each other
when the power goes out.
en.editorNote: The 'modern enough / old-fashioned enough' frame is a clean parallel structure — strong card text, strong toast line, strong Instagram caption
variations: Modern enough to keep up with the times. Old-fashioned enough to keep the handwritten card. That's the whole wish.

items.sourceNote: 短句紅包 / 賓客留言本 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

以後的日子，
不求一帆風順，
只求風來的時候，
你們的船上還有彼此。
zh-tw.editorNote: 短到可以寫紅包、長到夠當留言——避開「一帆風順」的老套說法，反過來用「風來的時候」更有畫面感，記得住
variations: 不求沒有風雨，只求風雨來的時候，你們是同一艘船上的人。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

May the years ahead
not promise calm seas —
but promise this:
when the wind comes,
you're still on the same boat.
en.editorNote: Short enough for a card, deep enough for a guestbook entry. Flips 'smooth sailing' into 'same boat when the wind hits' — way more memorable
variations: Not wishing you a life with no storms — wishing you the same boat, every storm, all of them.

items.sourceNote: 現代誓詞 register（具體承諾＞抽象保證），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我不會跟你保證浪漫到老。

我會跟你保證的是——
下雨天會幫你拿傘那一邊，
你發燒的時候會去買稀飯，
你講笑話沒人笑的時候，
我會第一個笑。

這些事情，做一輩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞越具體越動人——避開「永遠浪漫」「天長地久」這種空話，直接寫三個會被記住的小動作，比任何抽象承諾都重
variations: 我不答應你浪漫，我答應你三件事：傘那一邊、發燒的稀飯、笑話響起的第一個笑聲。

en.content:
I'm not going to promise you a lifetime of romance.

What I am promising:
the wet side of the umbrella is mine,
the congee run when you're sick is mine,
and when your joke lands in silence,
I'll be the first to laugh.

For as long as we both live.
en.editorNote: The most moving vows are made of three small, visible promises — not abstract forever-talk. Pick the actions you'll actually do
variations: Three promises, not forever-talk: the wet side of the umbrella, the soup run, the first laugh at your worst joke.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑致詞 register（自嘲伴娘版），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
她大學時跟我說：
「我這輩子不結婚。」

後來她又說：
「結婚也可以，但對方要懂我。」

再後來她說：
「我找到了。」

所以今天我站在這裡——
用最大聲的方式，
證明她真的什麼都會反悔，
除了選你這件事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴娘致詞神級結構——三段「她說過的話」鋪陳，最後一句反轉收尾，全場會笑、新郎會被融化
variations: 她說過很多不算話的話，但她選你這件事，是她講過最算話的一句。

en.content:
In college she told me:
"I'm never getting married."

Later she said:
"Okay, maybe — but only to someone who really gets me."

Then she said:
"I found him."

So I'm standing here today
as living proof
that she changes her mind about everything
except choosing you.
en.editorNote: Maid-of-honor gold structure — three quotes from her past, one reversal at the end. Reliable laugh, reliable warmth, groom melts
variations: She's reversed every position she's ever held — except the one where she picked you. That one she's never wavered on.

items.sourceNote: 短句紅包 register，現代生活畫面，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
新婚誌喜。

願你們的早餐桌上，
永遠多一杯咖啡的份；
願你們的週末早晨，
永遠捨不得先起床；
願你們的人生劇本，
永遠有對方的角色。
zh-tw.editorNote: 三句排比、三個生活畫面——避開官式賀詞的死板，又比現代俏皮版正式一點，紅包、卡片、留言本通用
variations: 願多一杯咖啡、捨不得起床的早晨、永遠在彼此劇本裡的角色——三件小事，做一輩子。

en.content:
Congratulations on your wedding.

May there always be one extra coffee on the breakfast table.
May your weekend mornings be the kind nobody wants to leave first.
May every chapter of your life
still have a part written for the other one.
en.editorNote: Three parallel images of ordinary life — softer than a one-liner, warmer than a formal blessing. Works for cards, envelopes, and guestbooks
variations: One extra coffee, slow weekend mornings, a part written for each other in every chapter — that's the whole wish.

items.sourceNote: Mark Twain 「分享喜悅」概念，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有人說，
喜悅要找到一個人分享，才算數。

我看著你們這幾年——
小事像中獎、大事像升遷、
甚至只是一杯好喝的奶茶，
你們都會第一個傳訊息給對方。

那就是了。
剩下的人生，
請繼續第一個傳給對方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把名言翻成可以被驗證的具體畫面——「第一個傳訊息」這個動作所有人都做過，比直接引用Mark Twain更打動人
variations: 好的伴侶，是你發生任何事情，第一個想告訴的人。請繼續做彼此的第一個。

en.content:
Someone once said
joy doesn't fully count
until you share it with someone.

I've watched you two for years now —
small wins, big news,
even a really good bubble tea,
you always text each other first.

That's it. That's the whole thing.
For the rest of your lives,
please keep being each other's first text.
en.editorNote: Translates the Mark Twain 'shared joy' line into a concrete, verifiable habit — 'first text' is something every guest has done, which makes the line land
variations: The right partner is the one you text first when anything happens. Please keep being each other's first text — for life.

items.sourceNote: 兄弟姊妹致詞 register（手足視角），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我從小就知道她會是個難搞的姊姊。

她挑零食挑了二十年，
挑大學科系挑了三個月，
挑工作前後換了五次。

但是挑你——
她沒有猶豫過半秒。

所以你大概是我們家
唯一沒被她退貨的東西。
請好好珍惜這個身分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 弟弟 / 妹妹致詞神級結構——先吐槽姊姊「挑剔」，最後反轉成「但她選你沒猶豫」，全場會笑、新郎會被感動
variations: 她退貨過一百件衣服、三個男朋友、無數家餐廳，但她從來沒退過你。這就是她最確定的答案。

en.content:
I've known my sister my whole life,
and let me tell you — she's picky.

She picked her snacks for twenty years.
She picked her major three different times.
She picked her career path five.

But picking you?
Not a single hesitation.

So congratulations —
you are officially the only thing in this family
she's never returned.
Please take that title seriously.
en.editorNote: Sibling-of-the-bride speech gold — roast her indecisiveness, then pivot to 'but she chose you instantly.' Reliable laugh, reliable warmth, groom melts
variations: She's returned a hundred shirts, three boyfriends, and countless restaurants. She has never once returned you. That's the most certain she's ever been about anything.

items.sourceNote: 同事 / 主管致詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
在公司認識他這幾年，
我看過他熬夜趕專案、
看過他被客戶罵到笑出來、
也看過他開會中途偷看手機。

後來我才發現——
他偷看的訊息，永遠是同一個人傳來的。

那時候我就想：
這人是真的找到對的了。

敬你們。也敬以後上班，繼續是他偷看手機的理由。
zh-tw.editorNote: 同事 / 主管致詞最會破冰的版本——用「上班偷看手機」這個所有人都懂的辦公室細節當笑點，比官式賀詞溫暖一萬倍
variations: 在辦公室看一個人有沒有真的戀愛很簡單——看他開會時手機亮起來的瞬間，那個微笑藏不住。他這幾年的微笑，全部都是因為你。

en.content:
I've worked with him for years now.

I've seen him pull all-nighters,
laugh through getting yelled at by clients,
and sneak glances at his phone in the middle of meetings.

It took me a while to notice —
the messages he was sneaking looks at
were always from the same person.

That's when I knew:
this guy found the real thing.

Cheers to you both.
And cheers to many more meetings he can't focus through.
en.editorNote: Coworker / boss speech that actually warms the room — uses a universal office detail (sneaking glances at phone) instead of corporate-speak. Warmer than a formal toast, less risky than a roast
variations: You can always tell when someone's really in love at work — the way their phone lights up and they can't hide the smile. His smile, for years now, has been because of you.

items.sourceNote: 現代詩意 register（星座 / 共同閃耀概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你們以前是兩顆星。

各自亮、各自轉、
各自照亮自己頭頂的那塊夜空。

從今天開始，
你們是一個星座。

別人抬頭的時候，
會把你們連在一起看，
會用你們的形狀記路。

願你們的光，永遠連成一條線。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「兩顆星變星座」的比喻寫得有畫面——適合致詞中段轉煽情的段落，文青朋友、藝文圈賓客特別買單，比一般祝福多一個層次
variations: 從今天開始，沒有人會把你們當作兩顆星看了。你們會被連在一起，被用來記路，被一起記得。

en.content:
You used to be two stars.

Each one shining,
each one turning,
each one lighting its own patch of sky.

Starting today,
you're a constellation.

When people look up,
they'll see you connected.
They'll use your shape to find their way home.

May your light always draw the line between you.
en.editorNote: The 'two stars become a constellation' metaphor with a concrete payoff (people use your shape to navigate) — for the emotional pivot in a thoughtful speech
variations: Starting today, no one sees two stars when they look at you — they see one shape, one constellation, one way to find their bearings.

items.sourceNote: 短句紅包 / IG留言 register，台灣現代生活，原創

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

願你們從今天起——
外送一起點，
沙發一起癱，
爛劇一起追，
好事壞事都一起轉發給對方看。

這就是幸福的全部公式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 四個動作排比、超本地的台灣日常畫面——紅包寫得進、IG發得出、留言本貼得上，年輕朋友圈通用
variations: 外送一起點、沙發一起癱、爛劇一起追——三件小事做一輩子，就是答案。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

From today onwards, may you —
order takeout together,
slump on the couch together,
binge bad shows together,
and forward every dumb meme straight to each other first.

That's the whole formula for happiness.
en.editorNote: Four parallel everyday actions — fits in a card, lands as an Instagram caption, signs a guestbook nicely. Modern, warm, no Hallmark energy
variations: Same takeout, same couch, same trashy show, same first-recipient for every meme. That's the whole formula.

items.sourceNote: 緊張開場 register，自嘲式破冰，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他們叫我講三分鐘。

我準備了八頁。

所以接下來——
要嘛你們聽我講完，
要嘛我老婆把我從台上拉下來。

大家可以一起賭，看誰先動。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞最緊張的前三十秒——用「我準備太多」當笑點，順便把「老婆會出手」帶進來，賓客會立刻放鬆，新人會偷笑
variations: 他們說致詞要短。我準備了長的。所以今天請大家陪我，一起測試一下這場婚宴的耐心極限。

en.content:
They told me to keep this short.

I wrote eight pages.

So here's the deal —
either you sit through all of it,
or my wife yanks me off this stage.

Feel free to place bets on which happens first.
en.editorNote: First-thirty-seconds opener that defuses the room — self-deprecating, names the elephant ('this might be too long'), invites the audience in. Reliable laugh
variations: I was told to keep this short. I prepared the long version. Tonight we find out together how patient this room really is.

items.sourceNote: 「最好的一天往後退一格」反轉 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
今天是你們目前為止最好的一天。

敬以後——
願今天，
變成你們很多年後回頭看的時候，
那個「還不錯，但比起後來都算普通」的日子。

乾杯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒結尾的萬用反轉——把「最好的一天」變成往後最普通的一天，畫面感比「百年好合」強十倍，文青朋友會立刻買單
variations: 願今天是你們目前為止最好的一天，也是以後回頭看，覺得「還只是開始」的那一天。

en.content:
Today is the best day you've had so far.

Here's to that —
and here's to today
becoming the day you look back on years from now
and say:
"Yeah, that one was nice. But the ones after were better."

Cheers.
en.editorNote: A flip on the standard 'best day ever' toast — wishes the couple a future where today is the floor, not the ceiling. Lands well as a closer
variations: May today be the best day so far — and the most ordinary day looking back. Cheers to better ones.

items.sourceNote: Velveteen Rabbit「變成真的」概念，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
有一本童書說，
你被一個人愛很久很久，
久到頭髮都被摸到掉色，
久到眼睛都被看到黯淡——
你就會變成「真的」。

看著你們，
我覺得你們都已經有點掉色了。

那是好事。
那代表你們是真的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把絨毛兔的童話翻成婚禮致詞——用「掉色」比「變老」浪漫一千倍。適合認識新人很久的朋友、姊妹淘致詞，畫面感強、會被記得
variations: 被愛很久很久的東西會變成真的。看著你們，我覺得你們已經是了。

en.content:
There's a children's book that says
when you've been loved by someone for long enough —
long enough that your fur gets rubbed off,
long enough that your eyes go a little dim —
you become Real.

Looking at the two of you,
I think you're both already a little worn at the edges.

That's a good thing.
That means you're real.
en.editorNote: Velveteen Rabbit reference reworked into a speech — 'worn at the edges' is the keeper line, way warmer than 'growing old together.' For close friends or siblings
variations: Things that get loved long enough become Real. Looking at you two, I'd say you're already there.

items.sourceNote: 「同一個人愛很多次」現代誓詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我聽過一句話：
好的婚姻，
是跟同一個人，
戀愛很多次。

我答應你的就是這個——
會在你剪了頭髮之後，
再喜歡你一次；
會在你換工作之後，
再選你一次；
會在我們吵完架之後，
再愛你一次。

一輩子，要重複很多次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞高階版本——把「永遠愛你」拆成「會重複愛你很多次」，每一次都是新的選擇，比抽象承諾可信度高十倍
variations: 我答應你的不是「永遠」，是「再一次」——每次你變了一點，我會再選你一次。

en.content:
Someone said
a good marriage
is falling in love with the same person
over and over.

That's what I'm promising you —
the day you cut your hair,
I'll fall for you again.
The day you switch jobs,
I'll choose you again.
The day after we fight,
I'll love you again.

This is going to happen a lot.
I'm ready.
en.editorNote: Vow that reframes 'forever' as 'again, and again, and again.' Each fall-in-love is a new choice — way more believable than abstract eternity-talk
variations: Not promising forever — promising 'again.' Every time you change, I'll choose you over.

items.sourceNote: 「準時 / 洗衣」破冰致詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我認識他十年了。

十年來他從不準時、
洗衣服永遠分不出顏色、
手機鬧鐘要響十次才會起床。

直到他遇到她——
現在他比我準時，
衣服分得比我媽還細，
第一聲鬧鐘就坐起來。

所以我可以負責任地說：
這個女人，是奇蹟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎致詞萬用結構——三件「以前的他」搭配三件「現在的他」，最後一句封神。比直接稱讚新娘更有畫面、更好笑、更打動
variations: 她讓一個十年沒準時過的人開始準時，光這一點，就值得我們敬她一杯。

en.content:
I've known this guy for ten years.

Ten years of never being on time,
ten years of mixing whites with reds in the laundry,
ten years of needing ten alarms to wake up.

Then he met her.

Now he's punctual,
he sorts laundry better than my mom,
and he gets up on the first alarm.

So I can say this with full confidence:
this woman is a miracle.
en.editorNote: Best-man template gold — three 'before' bullets, three 'after' bullets, one closer line about the bride. Funnier than a direct compliment, lands warmer too
variations: She got a chronically late man to be early. That alone deserves a toast.

items.sourceNote: Robert Brault「給對方理由」概念，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
最幸福的事情是——

找到一個願意無條件愛你的人，
然後
用一輩子的時間，
給他一個又一個
值得愛你的理由。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞中段轉煽情的萬用金句——前後對稱結構，「無條件」與「給理由」的反差感很有力，文青朋友會立刻買單
variations: 被無理由地愛是運氣，花一輩子給對方理由是回禮——願你們兩件事都做得很好。

en.content:
The luckiest thing is
finding someone who loves you for no reason.

The most beautiful thing
is spending a lifetime
giving them reason
after reason
after reason.
en.editorNote: Adapted Robert Brault frame — the parallel structure ('for no reason / giving them reasons') is the engine. Strong pivot line for any thoughtful speech
variations: Being loved for no reason is luck. Spending a lifetime earning that love is the gift.

items.sourceNote: 「交出存摺」俏皮賀詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

從今天開始——
薪水請主動上繳，
密碼請即時更新，
零用錢請好好爭取，
吵架請見好就收。

婚姻成功的秘訣，
就是早點認清誰是老大。
zh-tw.editorNote: 台灣婚禮紅包傳統「認命派」賀詞——四句排比＋一句點題，年輕朋友圈傳得最快。寫卡片、IG、敬酒前都能用
variations: 薪水上繳、密碼更新、零用錢爭取——婚姻幸福的三大守則，恭喜你們提前準備好。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

From this day forward —
hand over the paycheck willingly,
update all passwords promptly,
negotiate allowances strategically,
and end every fight at the right moment.

The secret to a happy marriage
is figuring out early
who's actually in charge.
en.editorNote: The 'surrender your paycheck' template translated into a friendly toast — four parallel rules and one punchline. Works for cards, captions, and ice-breaker speeches
variations: Hand over the paycheck, update the passwords, pick your battles — congratulations on graduating to the advanced course.

items.sourceNote: 「現代 / 古典」二元 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
願你們的愛——

有手機訊號的便利，
也有寫信時代的耐心；

有外送平台的速度，
也有自己煮飯的溫度；

有現代社會的自由，
也有老派承諾的重量。
zh-tw.editorNote: 三組「現代 / 古典」對比——比單純引用古文有溫度、比純現代俏皮更有重量，致詞、卡片、IG發文通用，所有年齡層都能接收
variations: 願你們有手機的快、有書信的慢、有自由的廣、有承諾的深——一輩子都不單薄。

en.content:
May your love have —

the convenience of cell signal
and the patience of letter-writing;

the speed of food delivery
and the warmth of cooking together;

the freedom of modern life
and the weight of old-fashioned promises.
en.editorNote: Three modern/classic parallel pairs — more substantial than a single line, less stiff than a formal blessing. Lands for any generation in the room
variations: Fast signal, slow letters, hot meals cooked together, free lives bound by old promises — that's the whole wish.

items.sourceNote: 「目擊者」結尾敬酒 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
在座每一位，
今天都是目擊者。

你目擊了一個承諾。
你目擊了兩個人決定，
從今天開始，
要把彼此寫進自己的故事裡。

等很多年以後，
當他們回頭看的時候——
我們，就是那個證明過「他們真的開始了」的人。

敬今天。敬目擊。敬以後。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞最後舉杯的高階版本——把賓客從「來吃飯的」變成「故事的證人」，瞬間提升儀式感。文青、藝文圈朋友會立刻買單
variations: 我們今天不是來吃飯的，是來當見證人的。敬你們的開始，也敬我們有幸在現場。

en.content:
Every single person in this room
is a witness today.

You witnessed a promise.
You witnessed two people deciding
to write each other
into the rest of their stories.

Years from now,
when they look back —
we're the ones who can say:
"I was there. I saw it start."

To today. To witnessing. To everything after.
en.editorNote: Premium speech-closer — promotes every guest from 'someone eating dinner' to 'witness to a story.' Adds instant ceremonial weight, lands hard at any wedding
variations: We're not just here for the meal — we're the witnesses. To the promise, to the start, to all the years we get to point at this one.

items.sourceNote: 祖父母致詞 register（長年婚姻過來人視角），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
五十三年前，
我跟你阿嬤站在你們今天站的位置。

那時候我們什麼都不知道——
不知道會有幾個孩子、
不知道會搬幾次家、
不知道半夜會被誰的咳嗽叫醒。

但你阿嬤跟我說：
「不知道，才好玩。」

孫女、孫女婿，今天起，請好好享受那些還不知道的事情。
zh-tw.editorNote: 祖父母致詞最有殺傷力的版本——「不知道才好玩」是金句，用一輩子的婚姻當背書，全場必哭。也適合外公、外婆、長輩代表
variations: 我跟你阿嬤走了五十多年，最好的部分都是我們當初不知道的。願你們也擁有那些「還不知道」的日子。

en.content:
Fifty-three years ago,
your grandmother and I stood right where you're standing.

We didn't know anything yet —
not how many kids,
not how many moves,
not whose cough would wake us up at 2 a.m.

And your grandmother told me:
"Not knowing is the best part."

So from today on, you two —
please enjoy every single thing you don't know yet.
en.editorNote: Grandparent toast at its strongest — 'not knowing is the best part' is the keeper line, backed by a real long marriage. Works for any senior family member who's been married a long time
variations: Your grandmother and I have had fifty-three years of not knowing what's next. It's been the best part. May your years be just as unknown.

items.sourceNote: 繼親 / 重組家庭誓詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你的，不只是你。

我也答應你身邊那兩個小朋友——

我不會搶走他們原本就有的位子，
我會在他們旁邊，多加一個位子。

他們難過的時候，
我會陪一陣子；
他們開心的時候，
我會跟著笑久一點。

從今天起，
我們是一個更大的家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 重組家庭誓詞最重要的原則——「不取代、只加位子」。避開「我會當你們的新爸爸/媽媽」這種敏感說法，改用「多一個位子」更安全也更動人
variations: 我不是來取代誰的，我是來多加一張椅子的——多一個人陪你們笑、多一個人陪你們哭。

en.content:
I'm not just promising you.

I'm also promising the two small humans
standing next to you —

I won't take anyone's place.
I'll just pull up another chair beside the ones you already have.

When you're sad,
I'll sit with you for a while.
When you're happy,
I'll laugh a little longer with you.

Starting today,
we're a bigger family.
en.editorNote: Blended-family vow at its strongest — 'pull up another chair' is the keeper image. Avoids the loaded 'new mom/dad' framing and lands softer with the kids in the room
variations: Not taking anyone's place — just adding a chair. One more person to laugh with, one more person to sit with when it's hard.

items.sourceNote: 現代短句紅包 register（捷運 / 通勤畫面），原創

zh-tw.content:
新婚誌喜。

願你們以後——
搭捷運永遠有兩個位子坐在一起，
下大雨永遠有一個人記得帶傘，
回家永遠有一盞燈為對方留著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 三句排比、三個超本地的台灣生活畫面（捷運、下雨、留燈）——紅包寫得進、IG發得出，比官式賀詞溫暖、比現代俏皮版更詩意
variations: 願你們有並排的座位、共用的傘、回家路上那盞為對方留的燈——一輩子都不孤單。

en.content:
Congratulations on your wedding.

May you always —
find two seats next to each other on the train,
have one of you remember the umbrella,
and come home to a light left on by the other.
en.editorNote: Three parallel images from ordinary commuter life — seats, umbrella, porch light. Works for cards, captions, and guestbooks; warmer than a formal blessing, more grounded than a punchline
variations: Side-by-side seats, one umbrella between you, a light left on at home — that's the whole wish.

items.sourceNote: 好友短敬酒 register（簡短有力），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我講三句話。

第一，我認識他們很多年。
第二，他們是真的。
第三，請大家舉杯——

敬「真的」這件事。
敬今天讓我們全部都看見它的兩個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 短到三十秒就講完的萬用敬酒——三句話結構、最後一句封神。適合排在後段、賓客已經累了的時候上場，比長篇大論更有效
variations: 三句話：我認識他們、他們是真的、請大家舉杯。敬真的。

en.content:
Three sentences.

One: I've known these two a long time.
Two: they're the real thing.
Three: please raise your glass —

to the real thing.
And to the two people who showed all of us what it looks like today.
en.editorNote: Thirty-second toast that always works — three-sentence structure, one closer. Best deployed late in the program when the room is tired of long speeches
variations: Three lines: I've known them forever, they're the real deal, raise your glass. To the real thing.

items.sourceNote: 交友軟體 / 滑卡時代 register，2026 現代俏皮，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
他們的愛情故事，
是這個年代最童話的版本——

男生右滑，
女生右滑，
他們先見了面確認對方不是詐騙集團，
再見面確認對方不是已讀不回的人，
第三次見面確認對方是會把照片洗出來的人。

然後就走到了今天。

敬演算法。
更敬，那些演算法之後，自己決定的事情。
zh-tw.editorNote: 致詞中段笑點＋反轉用——交友軟體是2026最有共鳴的婚禮笑點之一。先吐槽現代速食愛情，最後一句反轉到「演算法之後是自己的選擇」，年輕賓客全場買單
variations: 他們是滑出來的、見出來的、走出來的——演算法只是開頭，剩下都是他們自己寫的。

en.content:
Their love story is the most modern fairytale there is —

Boy swiped right.
Girl swiped right.
They met up to confirm neither was a catfish,
met again to confirm neither was a ghoster,
and met a third time to confirm the other actually prints photos.

And here we are.

To the algorithm.
And more importantly —
to everything they decided for themselves after it.
en.editorNote: Best 2026 speech opener for app-met couples — roasts swipe culture, then pivots to 'the algorithm only started it, they wrote the rest.' Lands hard with anyone under 40 in the room
variations: He swiped, she swiped, they showed up. The algorithm gets credit for the intro — they get credit for everything else.

items.sourceNote: 超具體小承諾誓詞 register（生活細節），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你六件事——

你忘記帶鑰匙的時候，我會回家開門。
你想吃宵夜的時候，我會說好。
你模仿你媽講話的時候，我會永遠覺得超好笑。
你講超長故事的時候，我會聽完。
爐火滅掉的時候，我會去重新點燃。
我們吵架的時候，我會是先說「我們聊聊」的那個。

這六件事。一輩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞最高招——六個具體到不像話的小動作，比任何抽象承諾都有重量。注意：剛好六件，再多會稀釋；每一件都是「對方會點頭：對，這就是他/她」的細節
variations: 我答應你的不是浪漫，是六件小事：開門、宵夜、笑你模仿你媽、聽你講長故事、重新點火、先說「我們聊聊」。

en.content:
I'm making you six promises —

When you forget your keys, I'll come home to open the door.
When you want late-night snacks, the answer is yes.
When you do the impression of your mom ordering at a restaurant, I will laugh every single time.
When your story runs forty-five minutes, I'll listen to all of it.
When the fire goes out, I'll be the one to restart it.
When we fight, I'll be the first one to say "let's talk."

Six things. For life.
en.editorNote: Top-tier vow structure — exactly six concrete, character-revealing promises. Specificity is the magic; abstract 'forever' vows can't compete. Cap at six or impact dilutes
variations: Six promises, not forever-talk: open the door, say yes to snacks, laugh at the impressions, listen to the long stories, restart the fire, speak first after the fight.

items.sourceNote: 遠端工作 / 居家 register（Zoom 笑點），2026 現代敬酒，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我聽說他們兩個都在家工作。

那我先在這邊敬一杯——

敬以後五十年，
每天聽對方用「會議的聲音」開會，
還是覺得對方很可愛。

這個難度，
比愛情本身還高。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 婚禮最現代的笑點——居家工作 / Zoom 語氣是所有人都共鳴的場景。短、準、會笑，敬酒前段或中段都能用
variations: 敬遠距上班的你們——願你們五十年後，還是覺得對方「開會的聲音」很迷人。

en.content:
I hear these two both work from home.

So I want to raise a glass —

to the next fifty years
of hearing each other's "Zoom voice" every single day,
and still thinking it's adorable.

That's a harder vow than the actual vows.
en.editorNote: Quintessential 2026 toast joke — work-from-home and Zoom-voice land with everyone who lived through the last few years. Short, sharp, reliable laugh
variations: To both of you working from home — may his Zoom voice never lose its charm. That's the real test.

items.sourceNote: 「選擇我們」現代誓詞 register（dating after marriage 概念），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你：

大的時候我會在，
小的時候我也會在。

旅行的時候我會牽你的手，
洗衣服的時候我也會牽你的手。

結婚以後，
我會繼續約你出去。

不會把「我們」當成已完成的事——
會把「我們」當成每天都要重新選一次的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代誓詞最重要的觀念——「結婚後還要繼續約會」。把「永遠」改成「每天重新選一次」，比抽象承諾真實一百倍
variations: 我答應你的不是終點線，是起跑線——婚後我會繼續約你出去，繼續每天重新選擇「我們」。

en.content:
I promise you this:

I'll be there for the big moments,
and I'll be there for the small ones.

I'll hold your hand when we're traveling,
and I'll hold your hand when we're doing laundry.

After we're married,
I'll keep asking you out on dates.

I won't treat "us" as something we finished today —
I'll treat "us" as something we choose, on purpose, every single morning.
en.editorNote: Modern vow centered on the keeper concept: keep dating after marriage. Reframes 'forever' as 'choosing us daily' — far more believable than abstract eternity-talk
variations: Not promising forever — promising to keep dating you, keep choosing us, keep showing up on purpose every single day.

items.sourceNote: 「保持好奇」現代誓詞 register（不假設已經懂對方），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你最重要的一件事——

我永遠不會以為我已經懂你了。

你三十歲的樣子、
四十歲的樣子、
五十歲哪天突然想學陶藝的樣子，
我都會用第一次認識你的那種好奇，
重新認識一次。

婚姻不是把你看完，
是繼續想看下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞最被低估的承諾——「不假設我已經懂你」。把婚姻寫成「持續的好奇」而不是「終於了解一個人」，比抽象承諾更現代、更耐用
variations: 我不會假設我已經認識你。我會用一輩子，繼續好奇你下一個版本長什麼樣子。

en.content:
Here's the most important thing I'm promising you —

I will never assume I've already figured you out.

The version of you at thirty,
at forty,
the version that randomly decides to learn pottery at fifty —
I'll meet each one
with the same curiosity I had the first time I met you.

Marriage isn't finishing the book.
It's wanting to keep reading.
en.editorNote: Most underrated vow — promising not to assume you already know your partner. Reframes marriage as ongoing curiosity instead of 'finally understanding someone.' More modern, more durable
variations: I won't assume I already know you. I'll spend a lifetime staying curious about whoever you're becoming next.

items.sourceNote: 「故事＋承諾」誓詞 register（具體事件當錨點），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我永遠記得——
那次你把洗碗精倒進洗碗機，
泡泡淹滿整個廚房，
你站在門口看著我，
那個表情我這輩子忘不了。

我笑到沒辦法生氣，
然後我們一起跪在地上擦泡泡。

那天我就決定了：
以後不管你弄壞什麼，
我都會一起跪下來擦。

這就是我的誓言。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞最高招——一個具體故事＋一個從中長出來的承諾。比抽象承諾真實一百倍，因為對方點頭那一刻，全場都會跟著被打動
variations: 你弄翻過、弄壞過、弄丟過很多東西——以後再弄壞什麼，我都會一起跪下來擦。

en.content:
I'll never forget —
the night you put dish soap in the dishwasher
and flooded the kitchen with bubbles,
and you just stood in the doorway looking at me
with a face I'll remember for the rest of my life.

I laughed too hard to be mad.
Then we got down on our knees together
and wiped up the foam.

That's the night I knew:
whatever you break,
I'll get down on the floor with you and clean it up.

That's my vow.
en.editorNote: Top-shelf vow structure — a specific story with a promise grown out of it. More believable than abstract forever-talk because the partner nods and the whole room nods with them
variations: You've spilled, broken, and flooded plenty of things. Whatever's next — I'll get down on the floor with you and clean it up.

items.sourceNote: 「愛你的怪癖」幽默誓詞 register，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你——

會一輩子愛你「永遠把襪子脫在沙發旁邊」這件事。
會一輩子愛你「半夜偷搶整條棉被」這件事。
會一輩子愛你「點完外送又改主意」這件事。
會一輩子愛你「把我的乳液當你的乳液」這件事。

不是「忍受」，是真的愛。

因為這些，才是你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞反向操作——不是發誓忍受對方缺點，是發誓愛上對方缺點。比「我會包容你」更高招，因為「愛」跟「忍」差很遠
variations: 我不答應忍受你的怪癖，我答應愛上它們——因為那就是你的版本，沒有人有。

en.content:
I promise to love you —

including the socks you leave next to the couch every single night.
Including the way you steal the entire blanket at 3 a.m.
Including the takeout orders you change three times after submitting.
Including the way you treat my moisturizer like community property.

Not tolerate. Love.

Because those are the parts that make you, you.
en.editorNote: Inverted vow structure — instead of vowing to tolerate the quirks, vowing to actually love them. Stronger than 'I'll accept your flaws' because love and tolerance are very different things
variations: Not promising to put up with your weird stuff — promising to love it. Couch socks, blanket theft, moisturizer raids and all.

items.sourceNote: 「人生VIP升級」現代俏皮賀詞 register，台灣本地，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
新婚快樂。

恭喜你們正式升級為——

人生 VIP 雙人方案。
含外送專屬折扣（自己出）、
冷氣遙控器共享權（要搶）、
爸媽雙倍紅包（雙倍壓力）、
以及一張不能取消的終身會員卡。

方案費用：愛。
方案期限：一輩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「結婚」翻成 SaaS 訂閱方案的台灣現代俏皮版——四項條款＋一句點題，超適合年輕朋友圈紅包、IG 發文，比官式賀詞好笑十倍
variations: 恭喜升級雙人方案——終身會員、不能退費、唯一條款是繼續愛對方。

en.content:
Happy wedding day.

Congratulations on officially upgrading to —

the Two-Person Premium Plan.
Includes: shared takeout discounts (you're paying),
thermostat remote co-ownership (you'll fight for it),
double in-laws (double the holidays),
and a lifetime membership with no cancel button.

Plan fee: love.
Term: forever.
en.editorNote: 'Marriage as a SaaS subscription' — four bullet terms plus one closer. Lands hard with a younger crowd; perfect for cards, captions, group chats. Funnier than any formal blessing
variations: Congrats on the Premium upgrade — lifetime tier, no refund button, only requirement is continuing to love each other.

items.sourceNote: 「現在進行式」反轉敬酒 register（不講從此幸福），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
童話故事都用「從此過著幸福快樂的日子」當結尾。

但真實的婚姻沒有結尾——
只有一個又一個現在進行式：

現在在洗碗。
現在在吵衛生紙要正裝還是反裝。
現在在沙發上看劇看到睡著。
現在牽著對方的手過馬路。

敬今天。
敬以後一萬個普通的「現在」。
敬「從此」這兩個字，一直延續下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 敬酒結尾的高階反轉——把童話的「從此幸福快樂」拆解成現在進行式的具體畫面。比口號式祝福更有重量、文青朋友尤其買單
variations: 童話有結尾，婚姻沒有——只有一個又一個普通的「現在」。敬你們的每一個現在。

en.content:
Fairytales end with "happily ever after."

Real marriages don't end —
they just keep being one ongoing present moment after another:

Doing the dishes right now.
Arguing about which way the toilet paper roll faces right now.
Falling asleep on the couch mid-show right now.
Holding hands across a crosswalk right now.

To today.
And to ten thousand ordinary 'right nows' after this one.
To 'ever after' just being a long string of those.
en.editorNote: High-end speech closer — unpacks the fairytale 'ever after' into a string of concrete present-tense moments. Heavier than slogans, lands especially hard with a thoughtful crowd
variations: Fairytales end. Real marriages just keep going — one ordinary 'right now' after another. Cheers to every one of yours.

items.sourceNote: 「我答應你比 X 更愛你」幽默誓詞 register（比較式發誓），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你——

會比愛睡覺更愛你（這真的很難，你知道我多愛睡）。
會比愛吃宵夜更愛你（這也很難，你知道我多愛吃）。
會比愛刷手機更愛你（這最難，你知道我多廢）。

我承認我把標準設得很高，
但你值得。

我會努力，每天努力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 比較式誓詞——把自己最愛的東西排出來，然後說對方贏。用「自黑」當底色比直接說「我超愛你」更可信、也更好笑
variations: 我答應比愛我自己更愛你——這對一個自戀的人來說，是我能給的最高承諾。

en.content:
I promise to love you —

more than I love sleep (and you know how much I love sleep).
More than I love late-night snacks (and you know how much I love those).
More than I love doom-scrolling my phone (and you know how bad I am at putting it down).

I know I'm setting the bar painfully high.

But you're worth it.

I'll keep trying. Every single day.
en.editorNote: Comparison-style vow — list the things you actually love, then say your partner wins. Self-roasting underneath makes 'I love you' land more believable than declaring it straight
variations: I vow to love you more than I love myself — which, for someone as vain as me, is the biggest promise I've got.

items.sourceNote: 現代伴郎開場 register（吐槽自己＋抬高新人），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
大家好，我是伴郎。

新郎挑我當伴郎的時候說：
「你是我認識最會講話的朋友。」

我現在才明白——
他不是在誇我，
他是在警告大家。

所以今天我發誓：
講三分鐘。
不爆黑歷史。
不哭。

以上三條我至少會違反一條，
請各位有心理準備。
zh-tw.editorNote: 伴郎開場的安全牌——先自嘲＋下三個保證（同時暗示一定會破功），讓聽眾笑著放下期待。比「大家好我是新郎的好朋友」這種開場高十倍
variations: 新郎讓我當伴郎，不是因為我會講話，是因為我知道太多了——所以這篇致詞會非常、非常克制。

en.content:
Hi everyone, I'm the best man.

When the groom asked me to do this, he said:
"You're the best talker I know."

It just hit me —
that wasn't a compliment.
That was a warning. To all of you.

So here are my three promises tonight:
Under three minutes.
No embarrassing stories.
No crying.

I will break at least one of these.
Probably all three.
Buckle in.
en.editorNote: Best-man opener that opens with self-roast plus three 'safety' promises (that you're clearly about to break). Lowers the room's expectations and earns laughs without trashing the groom
variations: He picked me as best man not because I'm a great speaker — because I know too much. Which is why this speech will be extremely, extremely restrained.

items.sourceNote: 新人答謝＋敬父母 register（中文婚禮主場），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
謝謝爸、媽。

你們花了三十年，
把一個哭著到這個世界的小孩，
養成今天能笑著走紅毯的人。

從今天起，
我們會把這份愛接著傳下去——
傳給對方，
以後也傳給我們的家。

你們把我交到他/她手上，
我會把這份信任，
好好放在心上。

爸、媽，謝謝你們。
這杯，敬你們。
zh-tw.editorNote: 新人在敬父母環節的核心稿——「從哭著來到笑著走紅毯」這句意象抓得很穩，比「養育之恩」抽象詞彙打動人。三十秒能講完，又有畫面
variations: 謝謝你們把我從一個哭著來的小孩，養成今天笑著走紅毯的大人。這份愛，我會接著傳下去。

en.content:
Thank you, Mom. Thank you, Dad.

You spent thirty years
turning a baby who cried their way into this world
into the person walking down the aisle today, smiling.

From today on,
we'll keep passing that love forward —
to each other,
and one day, to the family we build.

You trusted me to them.
I'll carry that trust
for the rest of my life.

Mom, Dad. Thank you.
This one's for you.
en.editorNote: Toast-to-parents centerpiece — the 'cried in, smiled out' arc is the load-bearing image, more concrete than abstract 'thank you for raising me' lines. Reads in 30 seconds and lands visually
variations: Thank you for turning a kid who cried their way into this world into someone walking down the aisle smiling. I'll keep passing that love forward.

items.sourceNote: 「我選你」承諾 register（重點不是承諾「永遠」而是承諾「持續選擇」），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我不能答應你「永遠」。
沒有人能。

但我可以答應你——

明天早上起床，我選你。
下個月薪水到帳，我選你。
明年看了世界一圈、認識更多人之後，我選你。
十年後、二十年後、頭髮都白了之後，
我還是選你。

婚姻不是一個承諾，
是一萬個重複的選擇。

我會一個一個，慢慢選。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞最高階寫法——拒絕「永遠」這個無法兌現的詞，改用「持續選擇」這個能兌現的動作。比「我會永遠愛你」誠實一百倍，也重一百倍
variations: 我不答應「永遠」，我答應「明天再選你一次」——然後後天、大後天、十年後，都再選一次。

en.content:
I can't promise you forever.
No one can.

But here's what I can promise —

Tomorrow morning when I wake up, I choose you.
Next month when payday hits, I choose you.
Next year, after seeing more of the world and meeting more people, I still choose you.
Ten years from now, twenty, when our hair has gone gray,
I'm still choosing you.

Marriage isn't one promise.
It's ten thousand repeated choices.

I'll make them, one at a time.
en.editorNote: Top-shelf vow move — refuse the word 'forever' (which no one can actually deliver) and trade it for 'keep choosing,' which is something you can. A hundred times more honest than 'I'll love you forever,' and a hundred times heavier
variations: I won't promise forever. I'll promise to choose you tomorrow — then the day after, then ten years from now. One choice at a time.

items.sourceNote: Netflix 演算法比喻 register（現代俏皮致詞），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
真愛是什麼？

是把 Netflix 密碼給對方，
而且他亂點之後，
你不會在意演算法被搞亂。

是把同一個冰箱、同一張沙發、
還有一個會打呼的人，
當成你以後二十年的家。

恭喜你們，
找到願意一起共用演算法的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代伴郎伴娘致詞神器——「演算法被搞亂」是 2026 年最有共鳴的婚姻比喻之一，比「兩人合而為一」這種陳腔濫調好笑十倍，又意外地溫暖
variations: 婚姻就是：把 Netflix 密碼、冰箱、沙發、還有一個會打呼的人，全部當成「家」的一部分——而且還甘願。
真愛是共用演算法，是同一張沙發，是即使他把你的「為你推薦」搞亂了，你還是想跟他一起看完那一集。

en.content:
What is true love?

It's sharing your Netflix password
and not getting mad
when they wreck your algorithm.

It's the same fridge,
the same couch,
and one person who snores
becoming the shape of your next twenty years.

Congratulations —
you found someone
worth sharing the algorithm with.
en.editorNote: Go-to modern best-man / maid-of-honor opener — the Netflix algorithm joke is the most relatable marriage metaphor of 2026, gets the laugh, and somehow lands warm. Beats 'two becoming one' by a mile
variations: Marriage is one fridge, one couch, and one person who snores becoming the shape of your next twenty years. And being weirdly happy about it.
True love is sharing the algorithm, the couch, and the remote — and still wanting to watch the next episode together even after they ruined your recommendations.

items.sourceNote: 「具體小事」誓詞 register（拒絕宏大承諾，改用日常細節），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我答應你的不是大事。

是早上把你的咖啡先沖好。
是你加班那天，先把客廳燈留著。
是你想看的爛片，我陪你看完。
是吵架隔天，我先說早安。

大承諾人人都會說。
我答應你的是這些——
小到沒人看見、
但你會在我身邊感覺到的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 誓詞最動人的不是「我會永遠愛你」，是「我會幫你沖咖啡」——這版用一連串具體日常小事取代抽象承諾，每一句都能在五年後實際兌現
variations: 我答應你的不是「永遠」，是「明天早上的咖啡」、「加班那晚的燈」、「吵架隔天的早安」——這些你會真的摸得到的事。
宏大的承諾我留給電影。我留給你的，是每天那些小到沒人看見、但你會在我身邊感覺到的事。

en.content:
What I'm promising you isn't the big stuff.

It's your coffee, made first, in the morning.
It's the living room light, left on, when you work late.
It's the bad movie you want to watch — I'm watching it with you.
It's the morning after a fight — I'll say good morning first.

Anyone can promise the big things.
What I'm promising you are these —
small enough no one else will see them,
but close enough that you'll feel them, every day, beside you.
en.editorNote: The most moving vow isn't 'I'll love you forever' — it's 'I'll make your coffee.' This trades abstract promises for a list of small, daily, deliverable things. Every line is something you can still keep five years in
variations: I'm not promising forever. I'm promising the coffee in the morning, the light left on at night, and the good morning the day after the fight.
Big promises are for movies. What I'm giving you are the small things — too small for anyone else to notice, close enough that you'll feel them every day.

items.sourceNote: 證婚人 / 長輩致詞 register（古典結構 + 現代口吻），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我認識新郎很多年了，
看他從一個吃飯都不會收碗的小伙子，
變成一個會主動問「妳要不要喝水」的人——

我知道，這個改變不是時間做的，
是身邊這位姑娘做的。

婚姻不會讓人變好，
是「值得的人」會讓你想變好。

今天兩位站在這裡，
不是因為承諾了什麼，
是因為已經彼此改變了——
而且都甘願。

祝你們，
一輩子都記得今天這個甘願。
zh-tw.editorNote: 證婚人致詞中段必殺——避開「百年好合」官腔，改用一個具體的「他變好了」觀察。長輩講這種話比講成語有重量一百倍，年輕來賓也聽得進去
variations: 我認識他這麼多年，看他從不收碗的小伙子，變成會問「妳要不要喝水」的人——我知道，這個改變是身邊這位姑娘做的。
婚姻不會讓人變好。是值得的那個人，會讓你想變好。今天你們站在這裡，是因為彼此已經做到了。

en.content:
I've known the groom for years.
I watched him go from a kid
who couldn't even clear his own dishes
to a man who asks, "do you want some water?"
before she even thinks of it —

and I know:
time didn't do that.
The woman standing next to him did.

Marriage doesn't make people better.
The right person makes you want to be better.

The two of you aren't standing here today
because of what you promised.
You're standing here
because you've already changed each other —
and you're glad you did.

May you remember that gladness
for the rest of your lives.
en.editorNote: Officiant / elder-toast centerpiece — skips the generic 'two souls united' opener for one specific observation ('he learned to ask if she wants water'). When an older voice says something this concrete, it lands harder than any proverb
variations: I watched him grow from a kid who couldn't clear his own dishes into a man who asks if she wants water before she thinks of it. That wasn't time. That was her.
Marriage doesn't make people better. The right person makes you want to be better. You two are standing here because you already did.

items.sourceNote: 「兩個獨立的人靠近」哲學 register（Rilke 風但口語化），重新創作

zh-tw.content:
我不想跟你「合而為一」。

我想跟你各自完整，
然後在彼此身邊，
還是完整的。

我想保留我自己的怪、
我自己的安靜、
我自己的深夜想法。

我也想，
每天看著你保留你的這些。

婚姻不是把兩個人融成一個，
是兩個完整的人，
選擇靠近——

而且每天，都還願意再靠近一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代誓詞最高階寫法——拒絕「兩個人變成一個」這種抹除自我的浪漫陳腔，改用 Rilke「兩個獨處互相守護」的概念但寫得很口語。給重視個人空間的伴侶剛剛好
variations: 我不要跟你「合而為一」，我要跟你各自完整、然後選擇靠近——每天都選擇再靠近一次。
婚姻最美的版本，不是兩個人融成一個，是兩個完整的人，每天都還願意再靠近一次。

en.content:
I don't want us to become one.

I want us to stay two whole people
who choose, every day,
to stand close.

I want to keep my own weird,
my own quiet,
my own late-night thoughts.

And I want to watch you
keep yours.

Marriage isn't two people
melting into one.
It's two whole people
choosing to step closer —

and choosing it again tomorrow,
and the day after that.
en.editorNote: Top-shelf modern vow move — refuses the 'two become one' cliché (which subtly erases the self) and replaces it with Rilke's two-solitudes idea, but in conversational language. Built for couples who value independence as much as love
variations: I don't want to become one with you. I want us to stay two whole people who keep choosing, every day, to stand close.
The best version of marriage isn't two melting into one. It's two whole people choosing to step closer — and choosing it again tomorrow.
